description_long: This object is a medieval-style mace with a long, cylindrical wooden handle wrapped in a textured grip, likely leather, for secure handling. The head of the mace is a large, dark metallic structure featuring multiple prominent, evenly spaced spikes radiating outward, designed for maximum impact. The transition between the handle and the head is reinforced with a metal collar, adding both durability and visual contrast. The handle is straight, with a rounded pommel at the end, providing balance and preventing slippage during use. The overall appearance is robust and utilitarian, with a dark, weathered finish on the metal and a reddish-brown hue on the handle. No visible markings or decorative engravings are present, emphasizing its functional design.
description: A spiked medieval mace with a dark metal head and a straight, leather-wrapped wooden handle. The head features multiple sharp spikes and a reinforced collar.
description_view_0: Angled view showing the full length of the mace, highlighting the spiked head and the textured handle.
description_view_1: End-on view directly facing the spiked head, emphasizing the radial arrangement of the spikes.
description_view_2: Reverse angled view displaying the opposite side of the mace, with clear visibility of the handle and head connection.
description_view_3: Straight-on view from the handle end, showing the full length and symmetry of the mace.
attribute_handle_type: Straight cylindrical wooden handle with a leather-wrapped grip, rounded pommel, and no visible curvature.
attribute_blade_design: Spiked mace head with multiple evenly spaced, conical spikes radiating from a central metallic core; no traditional axe blade or beard.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional mounting with the spiked head fitted and reinforced onto the handle with a metal collar.
